content.problemStatement:
Chef has a two-dimensional matrix **A** of dimensions **N** × **M**, (**N** rows and **M** columns). 

He calls the matrix **A** beautiful if there exist an **a**×**b** submatrix, such that all of its elements are equal. In one minute Chef can increase one element of the matrix **A** by 1. Now he asks you to find out minimum time he will need to make the matrix **A** beautiful?



Please note that sub-matrix of a matrix is a **continuous** rectangular block of the matrix. It can be denoted by two pair of indices **(x1, y1)** and **(x2, y2)** where **x1 ≤ x2**, **y1 ≤ y2**. The content of the submatrix will be all the cells **(i, j)** such that **x1 ≤ i ≤ x2** and **y1 ≤ j ≤ y2**.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains two space-separated integers N and M denoting the number of rows and columns in the matrix A, respectively. Each of the next N lines contains M space-separated integers denoting the i-th row of the array. The next line contains one integer Q - the amount of questions. Each of the next Q lines contains two space-separated integers a and b denoting the sizes of the submatrix sought.
content.outputFormat: For each question, output a single line containing the minimum time that Chef needs to make the matrix A beautiful (for parameters a and b from the question).

sampleTestCases.explanation: The submatrix of size 1x1 with all elements equal to 2 is already beautiful, so the minimum time required is 0.
The submatrix of size 1x1 with all elements equal to 1 or 2 or 3 or 4 is not beautiful. The minimum time required to make it beautiful is 1.
The submatrix of size 2x2 with all elements equal to 1 or 2 or 3 or 4 or 5 or 6 or 7 or 8 or 9 is not beautiful. The minimum time required to make it beautiful is 4.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a dynamic programming approach. We will create a 2D array dp where dp[i][j] will store the minimum time required to make the submatrix from (0,0) to (i,j) beautiful. We will iterate over all possible submatrices and for each submatrix, we will calculate the minimum time required to make it beautiful. We will use the concept of the sliding window to optimize the solution.
solution.timeComplexity: O(Q * N * M * a * b) - for each question, we are iterating over all possible submatrices of size a x b.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N * M) - for storing the input matrix and the dp array.
